_metadata.description: NPK requirements per crop in kg/ha for Myanmar conditions
sources: FAO Fertilizer and Plant Nutrition Bulletin No. 16
IRRI Rice Knowledge Bank — Nutrient Management
Myanmar Department of Agriculture recommended rates
ICRISAT technical bulletins for chickpea, groundnut
FAO Crop Fertilization Guidelines for SE Asia
_metadata.notes: Values represent recommended application rates, not plant uptake. Actual field rates vary by soil fertility and target yield.

rice.notes: IRRI recommended: 80-120 N, 20-40 P2O5, 20-40 K2O for irrigated lowland rice in Myanmar. Split N application: 50% basal, 25% tillering, 25% panicle initiation.

black_gram.notes: Legume — fixes atmospheric N via Rhizobium. Starter N only. High P demand for root nodule development. S improves protein content.

green_gram.notes: Similar to black gram. Shorter duration crop, lower total nutrient demand. P critical for early root establishment.

chickpea.notes: ICRISAT: 20 N + 40-60 P2O5 + 20-40 K2O. Deep-rooted legume. High P and S demand. Rhizobium inoculant recommended for new fields.

sesame.notes: Moderate N demand. S important for oil quality. Sensitive to excess N (lodging risk). Apply N in 2 splits.

groundnut.notes: ICRISAT: 10-20 N + 40-60 P2O5 + 20-40 K2O + gypsum at pegging. Ca and S critical for pod filling. Gypsum application at 250 kg/ha recommended.
